record_types.name: EP0_IN_BUFFER_CONTROL_Register

fields.name: LENGTH_0
fields.description:
Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be
used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when
writing the status back.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_0
fields.description: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 0.

fields.name: PID_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: LAST_0
fields.description:
Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set
to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host)
this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has
filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: FULL_0
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 1.

fields.name: LENGTH_1
fields.description:
Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be
used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when
writing the status back.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_1
fields.description:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous
mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous
endpoint.\n For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64
bytes.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 1.

fields.name: PID_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: LAST_1
fields.description:
Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set
to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host)
this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has
filled the buffer with data.

record_types.name: EP10_IN_CONTROL_Register

fields.name: BUFFER_ADDRESS
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a NAK is sent. Intended for debug only.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_NAK
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a STALL is sent. Intended for debug only.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_STALL
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_18_25
fields.description:
Trigger an interrupt each time both buffers are done. Only valid in
double buffered mode.

fields.name: ENDPOINT_TYPE
fields.description:
Trigger an interrupt each time both buffers are done. Only valid in
double buffered mode.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_DOUBLE_BUFF
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt each time a buffer is done.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_BUFF
fields.description: This endpoint is double buffered.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFERED
fields.description:
Enable this endpoint. The device will not reply to any packets for
this endpoint if this bit is not set.

record_types.name: EP6_IN_BUFFER_CONTROL_Register

fields.name: LENGTH_0
fields.description:
Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be
used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when
writing the status back.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_0
fields.description: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 0.

fields.name: PID_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: LAST_0
fields.description:
Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set
to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host)
this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has
filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: FULL_0
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 1.

fields.name: LENGTH_1
fields.description:
Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be
used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when
writing the status back.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_1
fields.description:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous
mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous
endpoint.\n For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64
bytes.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 1.

fields.name: PID_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: LAST_1
fields.description:
Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set
to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host)
this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has
filled the buffer with data.
